name: Frank Tate
abstract: Frank Eastman Tate (born July 18, 1943) is an American jazz double-bassist. He plays a five-string double-bass.

interviews.title: Frank Tate interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

works.t: When a Woman Loves a Man
When a Woman Loves a Man

concerts.label: Ronny Whyte Trio: An Evening with Ronny Whyte

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Because we had Frank Tate on bass and we had a George ... and we just played tune after tune after tune.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: Frank Tate played bass, and the drummer was Fred Stahl. Really great players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: Once with Frank Tate and Howard Alden, in Woods Hole near Falmouth Community Center. That was fun too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Bassist — I don’t know if you know bassist Frank Tate but this goes back 21 years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022
